LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO INC.'S APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF RELIES ON THE VERIFIED COMPLAINT AND ABLEMAN DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF THIS APPLICATION. (APPLICATION, ITEM 7(A), 7(B).) FIRST, THE COMPLAINT IS NOT VERIFIED. ASSUMING IN ARGUENDO THAT PLAINTIFF FILED A VERIFIED COMPLAINT, A VERIFIED COMPLAINT CANNOT SUPPORT AN APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER WHERE THE PLAINTIFF IS A CORPORATION. (CCP 446; SEE WEIL & BROWN, CAL. PRACTICE GUIDE: CIV. PROC. BEFORE TRIAL, 9:912.) SECOND, THE ABLEMAN DECLARATION IS A BOILERPLATE DECLARATION THAT DOES NOT AVER FACTS WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY TO SUPPORT THIS APPLICATION. (CCP 482.040.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT THE CLAIMS AGAINST MS. CASTILLO ARISES OUT OF HER TRADE, BUSINESS OR PROFESSION. (CCP 483.010(C).) THIRD, THE CREDIT APPLICATION IS NOT ATTACHED TO THE EXHIBIT C TO THE ABLEMAN DECLARATION. (ABLEMAN DECL., 9.) TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF FILED A SINGLE APPLICATION TO ATTACH PROPERTY AGAINST A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY AND A NATURAL PERSON, PLAINTIFF IS INSTRUCTED TO FILE SEPARATE APPLICATIONS. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-29-2013 Off Calendar

Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order
Off Calendar Jun-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was uncontested. PLAINTIFF SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO INC.'s Application For Right To Attach Order and Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment is Denied. Plaintiff relies on the verified complaint and Ableman Declaration in support of this application. (Application, Item 7(a), 7(b).) First, the Complaint is not verified. Assuming in arguendo that Plaintiff filed a verified complaint, a verified complaint cannot support an application for right to attach order where the plaintiff is a corporation. (CCP 446; see Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial, 9:912.) Second, the Ableman Declaration is a boilerplate declaration that does not aver facts with sufficient particularity to support this application. (CCP 482.040.) Plaintiff has not demonstrated that the claims against Ms. Castillo arises out of her trade, business or profession. (CCP 483.010(c).) Third, the Credit Application is not attached to the Exhibit C to the Ableman Declaration. (Ableman Decl., 9.) To the extent that Plaintiff filed a single application to attach property against a limited liability company and a natural person, Plaintiff is instructed to file separate applications. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order
Off Calendar May-24-2013 Continued To Jun-06-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO INC.'s Hearing on Application For Right To Attach Order is continued to June 6, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302, to give the moving party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party shall provide a courtesy copy of its papers by May 28, 2013, with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. The moving party's failure to provide the copies may result in the motion being denied or taken off calendar. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
